# services.scrutiny

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.scrutiny`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.scrutiny.collector.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the Scrutiny metrics collector. |
| `services.scrutiny.collector.package` | `package` | The scrutiny-collector package to use. |
| `services.scrutiny.collector.schedule` | `string` | How often to run the collector in systemd calendar format. |
| `services.scrutiny.collector.settings` | `open submodule of (YAML 1.1 value)` | Collector settings to be rendered into the collector configuration file. See <https://github.com/AnalogJ/scrutiny/blob/master/example.collector.yaml>. Options containing secret data should be set to an attribute set containing the attribute `_secret`. This attribute should be a string or structured JSON with `quote = false;`, pointing to a file that contains the value the option should be set to. |
| `services.scrutiny.collector.settings.api.endpoint` | `string` | Scrutiny app API endpoint for sending metrics to. |
| `services.scrutiny.collector.settings.host.id` | `null or string` | Host ID for identifying/labelling groups of disks |
| `services.scrutiny.collector.settings.log.level` | `one of "INFO", "DEBUG"` | Log level for Scrutiny collector. |
| `services.scrutiny.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Scrutiny, a web application for drive monitoring. |
| `services.scrutiny.influxdb.enable` | `boolean` | Enables InfluxDB on the host system using the `services.influxdb2` NixOS module with default options. If you already have InfluxDB configured, or wish to connect to an external InfluxDB instance, disable this option. |
| `services.scrutiny.openFirewall` | `boolean` | Whether to enable opening the default ports in the firewall for Scrutiny. |
| `services.scrutiny.package` | `package` | The scrutiny package to use. |
| `services.scrutiny.settings` | `open submodule of (YAML 1.1 value)` | Scrutiny settings to be rendered into the configuration file. See <https://github.com/AnalogJ/scrutiny/blob/master/example.scrutiny.yaml>. Options containing secret data should be set to an attribute set containing the attribute `_secret`. This attribute should be a string or structured JSON with `quote = false;`, pointing to a file that contains the value the option should be set to. |
| `services.scrutiny.settings.log.level` | `one of "INFO", "DEBUG"` | Log level for Scrutiny. |
| `services.scrutiny.settings.web.influxdb.bucket` | `null or string` | InfluxDB bucket in which to store data. |
| `services.scrutiny.settings.web.influxdb.host` | `string` | IP or hostname of the InfluxDB instance. |
| `services.scrutiny.settings.web.influxdb.org` | `null or string` | InfluxDB organisation under which to store data. |
| `services.scrutiny.settings.web.influxdb.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | The port of the InfluxDB instance. |
| `services.scrutiny.settings.web.influxdb.scheme` | `string` | URL scheme to use when connecting to InfluxDB. |
| `services.scrutiny.settings.web.influxdb.tls.insecure_skip_verify` | `boolean` | Whether to enable skipping TLS verification when connecting to InfluxDB. |
| `services.scrutiny.settings.web.influxdb.token` | `null or string` | Authentication token for connecting to InfluxDB. |
| `services.scrutiny.settings.web.listen.basepath` | `string` | If Scrutiny will be behind a path prefixed reverse proxy, you can override this value to serve Scrutiny on a subpath. |
| `services.scrutiny.settings.web.listen.host` | `string` | Interface address for web application to bind to. |
| `services.scrutiny.settings.web.listen.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Port for web application to listen on. |
